# Olga Heleno Walker

> MBACP integrative child and adolescent psychotherapist working with children, young people and families across York, Harrogate and North Yorkshire.

## About

Olga Heleno Walker is an MBACP-registered integrative child and adolescent psychotherapist (ages 4–25) working across York, Harrogate and North Yorkshire, as well as offering online sessions. Her practice is trauma-informed, with extensive experience of working with young people affected by adverse childhood experiences (ACEs).

She also works regularly with neurodivergent children and young people, whether their neurodivergence is suspected or formally diagnosed. In practice, this means the room, the pace and the format of a session flex around the child, rather than the child being expected to fit a standard talking-therapy model.

Olga also takes a systemic view of each child and young person. A young person's difficulties rarely exist in isolation from what's happening around them, and she works to understand each child within that fuller picture rather than treating the presenting problem on its own. This could mean working across multi-agency settings, liaising with schools, GPs, CAMHS, social workers and other professionals involved in a child's care, so that the support around a child is joined up rather than fragmented.

Olga trained through Place2Be in London, one of the UK's leading children's mental health charities, completing a Level 7 Postgraduate Diploma in Counselling and Psychotherapy for Children and Young People. She currently works as a school counsellor at St Aidan's Church of England High School in Harrogate, and has previously worked as a counsellor in both primary (ages 4–11) and secondary (11–18) schools in London.

Before retraining as a psychotherapist, Olga spent over a decade in the corporate world, in consultancy and banking. She is multi-cultural and has lived in over ten countries, a background that has proved useful in setting up a school counselling practice from scratch, and which gives her a broad frame of reference to draw on when working with children and young people.

## Who she works with

- Children and young people, ages 4–25
- Neurodivergent children and young people (suspected or diagnosed)
- Families seeking support for a child or young person

## Areas she works with

- Anxiety and worry
- Low mood
- Anger and frustration
- Trauma and adverse experiences
- Neurodivergence
- Bereavement and loss
- Family change
- Friendships
- Self-esteem
- School-related worries
- Emotional regulation

## Approach

- Trauma-informed — works from the assumption that behaviour makes sense in context, shaping how sessions are paced and endings are handled.
- Neurodivergence-aware — adapts session structure, sensory environment and communication style to the individual child rather than expecting them to mask or perform.
- Led by the child, with play as a tool — follows whichever route each child prefers to process, whether that's talk, play, drawing, building or movement.
- Integrative, not one-size-fits-all — draws on psychodynamic, attachment-based, cognitive-behavioural and mindfulness-informed approaches, led by the child's age, history and presentation.
- Systemic, not just individual — looks at the young person in the context of family, school and wider world, working alongside parents, teachers, GPs and social workers where helpful.
- Safe and consistent — offers a steady, confidential relationship and works alongside parents, carers and schools where necessary, while protecting the young person's confidence.

## Qualifications & Membership

- Registered Member, BACP (MBACP) — bound by the BACP Ethical Framework
- Level 7 Postgraduate Diploma in Counselling and Psychotherapy for Children and Young People — Place2Be in affiliation with the University of East London
- Level 3 Certificate in Counselling Skills for Working with Children — Place2Be
- Level 2 Award in Counselling Skills for Working with Children — Place2Be
- Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT) — The Oxford Mindfulness Foundation
- MLitt, Middle East Security (Distinction) — University of Aberdeen

## Training & Experience

- 2025–present: School Counsellor — St Aidan's Church of England High School, Harrogate
- 2023–2025: Level 7 Postgraduate Diploma in Counselling & Psychotherapy for Children & Young People — Place2Be
- 2023–2025: School Counsellor — AIM Academies Trust (primary and secondary)
- 2022: Level 3 Certificate & Level 2 Award in Counselling Skills for Working with Children — Place2Be
- 2018–2021: Head of People — Expert Edge (now Havas Market)
- 2011–2014: Senior Auditor — JPMorgan Chase & Co.
- 2009–2011: Strategy Analyst & Risk Assurance Manager — Lloyds Banking Group
- 2004–2009: Research Associate to Senior Strategy Analyst — Corporate Executive Board (now Gartner)

## Location

- York, North Yorkshire
- Harrogate, North Yorkshire
- Online sessions also available

## Contact

Enquiries can be submitted via the contact form at https://olgahelenowalker.com/#contact. The form is for general enquiries only and is not monitored urgently. In an emergency, contact 999 or the Samaritans on 116 123.
